patches.find: Schumann resonance suppression during geomagnetic storms is well-documented — storms inject particles into the ionosphere, increasing electron density and damping the cavity resonance. The dome claims this as a prediction, but the dome's own cavity formula predicts a ~22 Hz baseline, not the observed 7.83 Hz.
patches.replace: Schumann resonance does respond to geomagnetic storms, but published direction is mixed — Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 and Pazos et al. 2019 both report amplitude enhancement during storm conditions; other papers report suppression under different conditions. The dome claims ≥30% suppression but provides no equation that would select suppression over enhancement, and its cavity formula predicts a ~22 Hz baseline rather than the observed 7.83 Hz.
patches.rationale: c4 H1: The rewritten detail_evidence now explicitly refutes the backwards suppression mechanism, but the TLDR still asserts it. Rewriting TLDR to match detail framing (mixed literature direction, not uniformly suppression).

patches.find: Schumann suppression during storms is standard ionospheric physics. The dome predicts the wrong baseline frequency (~22 Hz vs 7.83 Hz), undermining its ability to claim the suppression mechanism.
patches.replace: Storm-time SR response is not uniformly suppression in the established literature. The dome predicts the wrong baseline (~22 Hz vs 7.83 Hz) and supplies no formula for direction or magnitude during storms.
patches.rationale: c4 H1: tldr_verdict asserted 'suppression is standard ionospheric physics' — the exact claim the rewritten paragraph 2 now says cannot be defended. Updated to match corrected detail framing.

patches.find: Schumann suppression is ionospheric physics; dome predicts ~22 Hz baseline, not 7.83 Hz.
patches.replace: Dome cavity geometry predicts ~22 Hz baseline, not the observed 7.83 Hz; storm-time SR direction is mixed in the literature, and no dome formula derives the claimed ≥30% suppression threshold or 6-hour window.
patches.rationale: c4 H2: Field actively contradicted its own detail_evidence after EXP-227 rewrite. First clause asserted suppression is ionospheric physics; detail_evidence now explicitly refutes this. Replaced with accurate summary matching current detail content.

patches.find: with no a-priori constraint that would have ruled out the opposite direction had the data come in differently.
patches.replace: with no a-priori constraint that would have ruled out the opposite direction had the data come in differently. Note the dome's account contains a structural contradiction: paragraph 1 cites the dome's own Live Power Dashboard reporting no G3+ storm observed since deployment, while this paragraph describes the dome's narrative of ~35% suppression during an observed G3+ storm. The March 20–22 2026 G3 event (NOAA SWPC confirmed, Kp=7) either triggered the monitor or it did not — the dome simultaneously presents both framings without resolving the discrepancy.
patches.rationale: ISS-1417 (c3 H4, c4 H4): Unscored-vs-observed internal contradiction not explicitly surfaced in text. Adding explicit contradiction sentence at end of V51.1 Update paragraph to close ISS-1417.

patches.find: paper most often cited for storm-time SR perturbation
patches.replace: paper often cited in the storm-time SR literature
patches.rationale: c4 H6: 'most often cited' is an unsupported empirical claim about citation frequency. Softened to defensible 'often cited in the storm-time SR literature' which conveys the same meaning without the superlative claim requiring citation metrics.

patches.find: adds no testable content over a coin-flip across the documented literature
patches.replace: adds no testable content beyond naming whichever combination of direction, magnitude, and time window the most recent observation happened to show — none of direction, magnitude, or time is fixed by dome geometry
patches.rationale: c4 H7: 'coin-flip' rhetoric oversimplifies PRED-SR-SUPPRESS which specifies direction + magnitude threshold (>=30%) + time window (6 hours). Replace with three-dimension framing that preserves the non-falsifiability criticism without caricature.
